title: Specify LintFinding
description: Wire shape for a single structured lint finding per RFC-28 §Structured lint finding schema. Shared by deterministic scanners (`specrun lint`), framework JSON export (`specdev check --format json`), target-adapter review briefs, model-assisted scorers, CI annotations, and dashboards. Field names are kebab-case at every nesting level. Producer-local `id` is distinct from the codex `rule-id` — `id` is a stable per-run handle (e.g. `FIND-0001`) and `rule-id` is the durable codex citation (e.g. `UNI-014`). Conditional requirements that JSON Schema cannot express (e.g. `confidence` required for `source: model-assisted`) are enforced producer-side.

id.description: Producer-local stable id for this run (e.g. `FIND-0001`). NOT the codex rule id; report-local heading ids such as `SEC-1` or `UNI-3` also live here when emitted as JSON.

rule-id.description: Codex rule id (e.g. `UNI-014`). Required when the finding maps to a codex rule; absent for findings that do not cite codex policy.

related-rule-ids.description: Additional codex ids that informed the finding.

target-adapter.description: Target-adapter name when the finding is adapter-specific.

source-adapter.description: Source-adapter name when the finding is source-extraction-specific.

slice.description: Slice name when the finding is slice-scoped.

location.description: File path plus optional line, column, and end positions. Omitted for findings without a single anchor location; per-file evidence may carry `locations[]` instead.

remediation.description: Concrete action to clear the finding.

confidence.description: Producer confidence. Producers MUST set `confidence` for `source: model-assisted`; the conditional rule is enforced producer-side, not by this schema.

fingerprint.description: Stable hash of `(rule-id, location, evidence-payload)` per RFC-28 §Structured lint finding schema fingerprint algorithm. Excludes producer-controlled fields (`id`, `title`, `severity`, `confidence`, `status`, `change`, `slice`, `target-adapter`, `source-adapter`).

status.description: Triage status. Omitted by raw scanners; populated by review reports or CI state.

findingId.description: Producer-local stable id. Examples: `FIND-0001`, `SEC-1`, `UNI-3`. Letters and digits with internal hyphens; ends with a numeric tail. Distinct from the codex `rule-id` namespace.

kebabName.description: Kebab-case identifier matching slice and plan naming. The RFC does not pin a regex for `slice` / `change`; this kebab shape is consistent with existing Specify naming.

relativePathPattern.description: Project-relative path. Absolute paths, URI strings, and `..` segments are rejected.

severity.description: Closed RFC-28 severity enum, ordered for sort stability (`critical < important < suggestion < optional`).

evidence.description: Evidence union per RFC-28 §Evidence union. Closed `kind` discriminator with one legal field set per branch. Evidence payloads have a 16 KiB cap after UTF-8 serialization (enforced producer-side, not by this schema). Findings MUST NOT include secrets, full prompts, model transcripts, or full source files.

value.description: Bounded verbatim excerpt for local code or prose evidence.

sha256.description: Hex-encoded SHA-256 digest of the underlying evidence.

locations.description: Optional contributing locations referenced by the digest.

data.description: Domain evidence payload (e.g. contract compatibility metadata). Producers must keep `data` bounded and secret-free.
